sanskrit: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ । ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि ॥
hindiTranslation: जयपराजय, लाभहानि और सुखदुःखको समान करके फिर युद्धमें लग जा ।  इस प्रकार युद्ध करनेसे तू पापको प्राप्त नहीं होगा ॥
englishTranslation: Treat pleasure and pain, gain and loss, victory and defeat alike. Then enter the battle; you will not incur wrongdoing.
essence: Equanimity frees action from the stain of panic and craving.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Stay steady through pleasure and pain, success and failure. Act from that balance, and your action will not bind you.
simpleInsight: Inner steadiness makes hard action possible without guilt or panic.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen. Krishna pushes him one step further: do not let pleasure, pain, victory, or defeat shake your mind. From that steadiness, fight without staining yourself.
modernRelevance: You open the email that could end a friendship or save one. Your hands shake, but the decision still has to be made. This verse asks for balance before the next move.
translationLiteral: सुखदुःखे (pleasure and pain) / समे (equal) / कृत्वा (having made) / लाभालाभौ (gain and loss) / जयाजयौ (victory and defeat) / ततः (then) / युद्धाय (for battle) / युज्यस्व (be engaged) / न (not) / एवम् (thus) / पापम् (wrongdoing) / अवाप्स्यसि (you will obtain)
